title: Playground Transfer Command
concept: A portability-centered command/search workspace for core contributors reviewing PRs, where GitHub, ZIP, local directory, browser save, Blueprint bundle, and database artifacts are treated as first-class routes with a dense results pane.
focus: import/export hub as the primary information architecture
command/search overlay plus selected-route results pane
portability ledger for inbound, storage, and outbound artifacts
warm editorial accents behind dense operational controls
flows: Search or filter portability routes to import from GitHub, export to GitHub, import a zip, download a zip, save in browser storage, save to a local directory, or download database.sqlite.
Start Playgrounds from Vanilla WordPress, WordPress PR, Gutenberg PR or branch, GitHub import, Blueprint URL, zip import, featured Blueprints, or the full Blueprint gallery.
Save a temporary Playground to browser storage or a local directory with visible file-copy progress, then browse saved and unsaved Playgrounds with rename and delete actions.
Use Site Manager tabs for settings, file browser, Blueprint editing, database tools, logs, Homepage, WP Admin, path navigation, refresh, export to GitHub, and zip download.
Copy, download, or run the current Blueprint bundle and browse all 43 Blueprint gallery entries by captured categories.
featureCoverage: create from Gutenberg PR or branch
import from GitHub after account connection
browse and filter Blueprint gallery entries
save temporary Playground in browser storage
navigate active WordPress paths with URL input
refresh the active embedded WordPress page
understand destructive reset and stored Save & Reload behavior
use File browser with create folder/file, upload, browse, and code editing affordances
